title: Viruses Purification of Perkinsus spp.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fb0g4","text":"The purpose of this protocol is to isolate and characterize viruses that infect P.olseni, P.marinus.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":80,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: First, Fill centrifuge bottles with 450mL Perkinsus Culture in the logarithmic growth phase.
Centrifuge bottles at 1500 g; 15 min at room temperature.
Transfer supernatant to a clean tube, and store at 4°C for further analyses.
Resuspend pellet in 10% initial culture volume supernatant in a 50mL Falcon Tube.

Centrifuge tubes at 1500 g; 15 min at room temperature. During this centrifugation, prepare 2.5X PBS buffer (2.5mL of 10X PBS Buffer in 10mL mQ water QS) with protease inhibitor cocktail (1mM Phenylmethanesulfonyl fluoride = 1.74 mg for 10mL of solution). This solution must be freshly prepared (the half-life of PMSF is short = 55min at pH = 7.5)
Transfer supernatant to a clean tube, and store at 4°C for further analyses.
Resuspend pellet in 4mL PBS/0.35 M NaCl) containing protease inhibitor cocktail.
Transfer the pellet resuspended in 2mL Eppendorf Tubes (1.5mL/tube).
Add 100mL Glass beads. We tried Sonication at differents times (15sec-->10min) with or without ice --> NOT worked
Disruptor Genie for 15sec .
Verify cell disruption : place a 20mL under light microscope. No cell disruption was observed after 15sec Disruptor Genie.
Disruptor Genie for 1min .
Verify cell disruption : place a 20mL under light microscope. No cell disruption was observed after 1min Disruptor Genie.
Disruptor Genie for 3min .
Verify cell disruption : place a 20mL under light microscope. Cell disruption was observed after 3min Disruptor Genie.
Centrifugation at 10,000 g; 30 min, 4°C
Removed the supernatant to clean 1.5mL Eppendorf tube, discard the pellet (cell debris)
Centrifugation again at 10,000 g; 30 min, 4°C To be sure to discard the cells debris well.
Removed the supernatant to clean 1.5mL Eppendorf tube, discard the pellet (cell debris)
Conserve the tubes at 4°C
creator: Mathieu Lazare
